description: Labs gain weight easily. Weigh weekly. Keep lean — you should easily feel ribs. Obesity accelerates hip/elbow dysplasia.
Monitor for morning stiffness, difficulty rising, bunny-hopping gait. Labs are high-risk for hip and elbow dysplasia.
Labs' floppy ears trap moisture. Clean weekly with vet-approved solution. Especially after swimming!
Double coat sheds heavily. Weekly brushing reduces fur and distributes oils.
Feed 2-3 smaller meals. No vigorous exercise 1hr before/after eating. Know warning signs.
GSDs are #1 breed for hip dysplasia. Watch gait, stair-climbing ability, rising from rest. Keep lean.
Heavy double coat — shed constantly, heavily 2x/year. Daily brushing essential. Known as 'German Shedders'!
Excess weight dramatically worsens hip/elbow outcomes. Keep lean — visible waist from above.
GSDs are deep-chested = high GDV risk. Feed multiple small meals. Limit activity around meals. Gastropexy recommended.
NO jumping off furniture. NO stairs without a gate. Use ramps. 25% of Dachshunds get IVDD — prevention is everything.
Extra weight = extra pressure on the spine. Keep Dachshunds lean — the single best thing you can do for their back.
Long floppy ears limit airflow. Check for wax, odor, or redness. Clean gently if needed.
Persians' flat faces cause tear overflow and eye discharge. Wipe gently with damp cloth daily.
Long, dense coat mats easily without daily brushing. Pay extra attention to armpits, belly, and behind ears.
PKD affects 40% of Persians. Increased thirst/urination is earliest sign of kidney decline. Monitor water intake and litter box output.
Flat face = brachycephalic airway. Watch for noisy breathing, snoring severity, or exercise intolerance.
